capability_questions.capability_name: Service exists and is in good condition

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have an authentication service?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether an enterprise authentication service exists

questions.prompt: Is the authentication service reviewed at least quarterly to measure its efficacy?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether the service is reviewed on a regular cadence for efficacy

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have a formal system that authenticates subjects to network and applications?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether a formal authentication system exists for network and applications
questions.evaluation_focus: Formal authentication system for network and applications

questions.prompt: Is the same authentication system used consistently across the enterprise (VPN, LAN, cloud, and SaaS)—or do separate locations or systems maintain siloed authentication?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether authentication is consistent enterprise-wide

questions.prompt: Does the authentication system support cloud identity providers and federated authentication (for example SAML, OIDC) for enterprise applications?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether cloud IdP and federation are supported
questions.evaluation_focus: Cloud identity provider and federation support

questions.prompt: Is authentication system configuration reviewed at least quarterly?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether authentication configuration is reviewed on a regular cadence

questions.prompt: Does the authentication system integrate with access control policy decision points (PDPs) and policy enforcement points (PEPs) for contextual, identity-centric access?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether authentication integrates with access control mechanisms
questions.evaluation_focus: Integration with access control decision and enforcement points

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have an IAM engineer or dedicated authentication owner to configure authentication policies?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether a dedicated authentication owner exists
questions.evaluation_focus: IAM engineer or authentication owner for policy configuration

questions.prompt: If authentication is siloed across locations, is there central ownership of authentication policy—or do separate locations have independent authentication administrators?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether authentication engineering is centrally managed

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise formalize and document KPIs and KRIs for the authentication service?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether authentication KPIs and KRIs are documented

questions.prompt: Are authentication KPIs and KRIs reviewed at least quarterly?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether KPIs and KRIs are reviewed on a regular cadence

questions.prompt: Do KPIs or KRIs track MFA coverage, phishing-resistant authentication adoption (for example passkeys/FIDO2), or passwordless rollout progress?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether modern authentication adoption metrics are tracked
questions.evaluation_focus: MFA and phishing-resistant authentication coverage metrics

questions.prompt: Do KPIs or KRIs track failed authentication rates, credential stuffing indicators, or suspicious login patterns?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether credential attack and failed auth metrics are tracked
questions.evaluation_focus: Failed authentication and credential attack metrics

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise formally require MFA as a second factor for all access, including LAN, VPN, cloud, application access, and privileged access?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether MFA is required enterprise-wide for all access types

questions.prompt: Are passwordless authentication methodologies (for example FIDO2 passkeys, certificates, or biometrics) documented and deployed enterprise-wide or in defined scopes?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether passwordless authentication is documented and deployed

questions.prompt: Is phishing-resistant MFA (for example FIDO2 security keys or passkeys) documented and deployed enterprise-wide or for high-risk users and applications?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether phishing-resistant MFA is documented and deployed

questions.prompt: Are MFA, passwordless, and phishing-resistant authentication configurations reviewed at least quarterly for improvement?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether modern authentication methods are reviewed on a regular cadence
questions.evaluation_focus: Regular MFA and passwordless configuration review

questions.prompt: Does the organization have a documented risk-based authentication policy that adjusts authentication requirements based on context?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether a risk-based authentication policy exists

questions.prompt: Can the authentication system require step-up authentication (stronger factors or re-authentication) for high-risk sessions or sensitive resources?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether adaptive step-up authentication is supported
questions.evaluation_focus: Adaptive step-up authentication for high-risk sessions

questions.prompt: Are device trust, geolocation, and behavioral anomaly signals integrated into authentication decisions?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether device and behavioral signals inform authentication
questions.evaluation_focus: Integration of device and behavioral signals into auth decisions

questions.prompt: Are service accounts and workloads authenticated using governed methods—not shared human passwords?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether service account and workload authentication is governed
questions.evaluation_focus: Governed service account and workload authentication

questions.prompt: Do API and machine-to-machine clients authenticate using standards such as OAuth 2.0 client credentials, mutual TLS, or certificate-based authentication?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether API authentication uses modern standards
questions.evaluation_focus: OAuth and API client credential authentication standards

questions.prompt: Are shared credentials for non-human identities prohibited and detected through policy or monitoring?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether shared non-human credentials are prohibited
questions.evaluation_focus: Prohibition of shared credentials for non-human identities

questions.prompt: Is there a formal documented process for logging authentication events—including user lifecycle changes and failed authentications—to the SOC?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether authentication logging to the SOC is formally documented

questions.prompt: Are authentication events transmitted securely to the SOC?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether authentication events are logged securely to the SOC
questions.evaluation_focus: Secure authentication event logging to SOC

questions.prompt: Are dynamic changes to authentication configuration captured and transferred to the SOC in near-real time?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether dynamic authentication changes are sent to the SOC

questions.prompt: Are anomalous authentication patterns (for example impossible travel, credential stuffing bursts, or token replay) detected and alerted in near-real time?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether anomalous authentication patterns trigger detection and alerting
